source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
5 friends talk about their collected <image1>. Xenia says: "I have an even number of pins", Zach: "Half of my pins are planets, Sue: "I don't have any moons", Paul: "I have more moons than stars" and Yvonne: "I have more stars than planets". Below are the collections of the 5 friends. Which set of pins belongs to Yvonne?
<image2>
extraction_notes: options field contains only option labels without descriptive option text

image_dependency: question_text says 'Below are the collections of the 5 friends,' so the mapping cannot be solved from text alone
the clues refer to visual pin categories (planets, moons, stars) that must be identified from the image collections
multi_step: there are 5 separate person-level clues that must be matched against 5 collections
solving requires combining multiple conditions such as parity ('even number'), proportions ('half of my pins are planets'), and comparisons ('more moons than stars', 'more stars than planets')
the task is not a single lookup; it requires elimination across candidates A-E using both text clues and image counts
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice with a single-letter answer ('E')
the stated clues are concrete and checkable against each visual collection by counting pin types
answer_present=true and choices are explicitly defined

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和主图像资产齐全，registry_passed=true，且未报告资产缺失问题。
图像依赖性高，但对应图像已存在，核心语义可被完整恢复。
multi_step_score 和 verifiability_score 均较高，样本具备较好的解题价值与可核验性。
虽有 fine_grained_visual_counting 风险，但这属于难度/标注成本风险，不构成核心语义缺失。
